mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index signals inflationary pressure, prompting expectations of tighter monetary policy that increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of months when CPIAUCSL rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding three hundred thousand signals robust labor demand and economic overheating, triggering short-term risk-on sentiment and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in over 55 percent of instances where PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 300 thousand.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate earnings growth and driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side constraints and economic slowdown, leading to negative equity returns as growth expectations deteriorate.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day returns in over 50 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the federal funds rate above zero point five percent triggers immediate repricing of duration-sensitive equity valuations, causing short-term negative returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS crosses the 0.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment combined with falling payroll employment indicates labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending expectations and driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Concurrent UNRATE rise and PAYEMS decline produces positive 20-day returns in over 55 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects strong aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate profit margins and sustained positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant positive correlation with 60-day forward returns at the 90 percent confidence level over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its twelve-month moving average signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, prompting risk-off positioning and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO trading below its 12-month moving average yields positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of triggered months.
